title: The Marathon Continues — Each One Teach One
thumbnail_prompt: American Red Nose Pitbull in sleeveless black coveralls, facing camera directly, low angle shot from below, urban rooftop at dusk, city lights behind, arms crossed, confident watchful expression, amber eyes glowing. Bold text 'THE MARATHON CONTINUES' in bone white with 'CONTINUES' in rust orange. Dark steel palette, rust-orange sodium vapor accent, heavy vignette, 2D cel-shaded style, 1280x720 16:9
description: Mama lost the house in oh-three. I stayed. Sleepin' in a Cutlass Supreme with a pit bull and a stolen laptop. This is the origin story. The anti-hero arc. The 'Each One Teach One' promise. Five things you can own starting from zero — before AI finishes the race without you.

scenes.narration_text: Mama lost the house in oh-three.
scenes.visual_prompt: Boarded-up row house at night, plywood screwed over windows, faded foreclosure notice stapled to front door, overturned porch chair, forgotten child's shoe on cracked walkway, dead potted plant by steps, sodium vapor streetlight casting rust-orange light on facade. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Wasn't no warnin'. Just a letter. Then a lock on the door. She went down south. Family in Louisiana said they had room. I stayed.
scenes.visual_prompt: Extreme close-up of heavy padlock and new chain on residential door handle, official eviction letter wedged in door jamb, envelope with county seal visible, peeling paint on door frame, sodium vapor streetlight casting harsh orange from above, shallow depth of field. Rust orange and dark steel only, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Seventeen. Sleepin' in a Cutlass Supreme with a pit bull and a stolen laptop.
scenes.visual_prompt: Red Nose Pitbull extreme close-up (P5), one amber eye visible, scar across left brow, still and watching, rust-orange light on copper coat. Dark steel background, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, heavy vignette, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Three years like that. Couches. Cars. Places nobody should be closin' they eyes. But that laptop... that changed somethin'.
scenes.visual_prompt: Red Nose Pitbull (P3 variant) lying on cracked concrete beside closed laptop, head on paws, one eye open, single sodium vapor light casting long shadow. Dark room, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Library WiFi. Community center when they'd let me in. Started readin' things I wasn't suppose to understan'. Tax codes. Lien laws. How credit actually work. How bail bonds make money. Who own what in which neighborhood, and why.
scenes.visual_prompt: Worn wooden desk in dark corner of public library after hours, laptop screen casting bone-white glow, IRS tax form and printed legal document visible on desk, hand-written notes on lined paper, library card beside laptop, stack of law books barely visible in shadow behind screen. No people. 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Same way some folk hear music — I started hearin' systems.

Tried to put folk on. Ran workshops outta barbershops. Explained how to fight an eviction. How to read a contract 'fore you sign your life away. Half them nights ended wrong. TTP drama. Somebody owed somebody. Guns where there shoulda been questions.
scenes.visual_prompt: Barbershop converted to makeshift classroom, folding metal chairs in semicircle facing a whiteboard, handwritten notes on whiteboard reading 'KNOW YOUR RIGHTS', photocopied handouts stacked on barber station counter, lease agreement pinned to mirror with tape, warm incandescent barbershop lighting. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: But the dog stayed. Through all that. He stayed.
scenes.visual_prompt: Red Nose Pitbull (P6) walking through empty urban street at night, mid-stride, sodium vapor streetlight casting long shadow behind. Cracked pavement, abandoned storefronts. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Ain't get a record deal. Ain't get a scholarship. Ain't get saved by nobody.
scenes.visual_prompt: Red Nose Pitbull stopped walking, head turned toward camera, one eye catching rust-orange light, ears folded back, scar visible. Hold shot. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: I just learned how things work. And I ain't never forgot what it felt like not to know.
scenes.notes: Volume drops. Emotional thesis of origin story.

scenes.narration_text: That's The Aftermath. Already lived through the collapse. That's why I see it comin' for everybody else.
scenes.visual_prompt: Red Nose Pitbull sitting at rooftop edge (P4 silhouette), back to camera, city lights below, orange sodium vapor glow, night, fog. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.
scenes.notes: Near whisper. Title drop. Fade to black, hold 3 seconds.

scenes.narration_text: Wasn't no colors on me. But I knew everybody who wore 'em. You grow up where I grew up, you don't choose a side — you just live in the crossfire. Half my block was blue. Other half was business. I stayed between the cracks.
scenes.visual_prompt: Urban intersection at night, two different graffiti tags on opposing walls of an alleyway entrance, sodium vapor light pooling on wet pavement between them, chain-link fence with torn fabric caught in wire, narrow walkway between tagged walls suggesting a path walked carefully, cracked pavement with old spray paint marks. No humans. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Some folk sold. Some folk used. I just watched. The money was fast. The consequences was faster. I seen dudes go from Jordans to jumpsuits in the same summer. Ain't sayin' I was an angel. Just sayin' I was strategic.
scenes.visual_prompt: Concrete stoop at night, single expensive sneaker abandoned on top step, empty cash-counting band beside it, cigarette still smoldering in ashtray, court summons paper crumpled and discarded, sodium vapor light casting long shadows down the steps. No humans. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Check-cashin' on Fridays. Rent-to-own for a TV I couldn't afford. No bank account 'cause nobody told me I needed one. Every dollar I earned went right back into somebody else's pocket. I was grindin' and leakin' at the same time. That's what they don't show you — you can work hard and still be broke if nobody taught you how money actually move.
scenes.visual_prompt: Check-cashing storefront at night, neon 'CHECKS CASHED' sign buzzing, iron security bars on windows, 'RENT-TO-OWN — NO CREDIT CHECK' poster in window, payday loan rate sheet taped to glass door, ATM fee notice ($3.50), receipts blowing across sidewalk in wind. No humans. Dark steel palette with neon accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: The system got plenty of folk who look like me. Decided early it wasn't gonna get me. Not 'cause I was better. 'Cause I was watchin'. And what I saw told me: get smart or get got.
scenes.visual_prompt: Multiple surveillance cameras on a single pole at urban intersection, chain-link fence with barbed wire below, concrete overpass in background, harsh cold blue-white floodlight from above, 'AREA UNDER 24HR VIDEO SURVEILLANCE' sign on fence, everything sterile and institutional. No humans. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: That stolen laptop became a library. A law school. A business plan. YGs think the goal is respect. Respect don't pay rent. What pay rent is knowin' how to make a dollar work while you sleep. I started learnin' that on a cracked screen in a parkin' lot.
scenes.visual_prompt: Close-up of cracked laptop screen glowing in dark room, multiple browser tabs visible showing business plan template, legal document, and spreadsheet with dollar figures, sticky notes on screen bezel with handwritten reminders, worn desk surface with coffee ring stains, warm bone-white glow illuminating scattered notes. No humans. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Got to a point where it wasn't about me no more. Family eatin' next month. Kids not repeatin' the same mistakes. Breakin' the cycle ain't a motivational poster — it's a Tuesday. It's choosin' the library over the corner every single day.
scenes.visual_prompt: Kitchen table at dawn, two plates set with simple breakfast, child's crayon drawing of a house taped to refrigerator, packed school lunch bag on counter, coffee mug beside open notebook with budget written in pen, morning light through thin curtains, calendar on wall with bills-due dates circled. No humans. Dark steel palette with warm dawn accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Started runnin' workshops again. This time smarter. Smaller rooms. Folks who actually wanted to learn. Showed 'em how to read a lease. How to file a dispute. How to stop payin' interest they didn't owe. Some folk listened. And them folk? They still out here. Building.
scenes.visual_prompt: Barbershop converted to small workshop space, three folding chairs in tight semicircle, whiteboard propped against mirror reading 'HOW TO FILE A DISPUTE', photocopied lease agreements spread across barber counter, highlighter and pen on top of documents, warm incandescent light from vintage barbershop fixtures, clippers pushed aside to make room for handouts. No humans. Dark steel palette with warm golden accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Everybody left at some point. The homies. The family. The women. Everybody got a reason. But the dog never did. It's about who stayed when you had nothin'. That's loyalty you can't buy. Can't fake. Can't replace.
scenes.visual_prompt: Empty concrete stoop at night, single bare porch light buzzing, two chairs but only one shows wear marks, ashtray with old cigarettes, faded welcome mat, dead plants in cracked pots, one window lit warmly from inside suggesting someone stayed, quiet street behind. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: I started understandin' somethin'. Ownership is everything. Own your mind, mind your own. Don't wait for somebody to hand you a lane — lay your own brick. Every day. One at a time. Eventually you look up and you got a wall. You got a foundation. You got somethin' they can't take.
scenes.visual_prompt: Small urban commercial plaza at golden hour, barbershop with hand-lettered 'ESTABLISHED 2006' on glass, clothing store with 'BLACK OWNED' sticker in window, hand-painted murals on brick between storefronts, warm golden light on clean sidewalk, small sandwich board sign outside with hours written in marker. No humans. Dark steel palette with warm golden accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

remotion_props.quote: **Ownership** is everything. Own your **mind**, mind your **own**.

scenes.narration_text: Spoke some things into the universe and they appeared. Not 'cause the universe owed me nothin'. 'Cause I put the work behind the words. Vision without labor is just a daydream. But labor without vision? That's a prison. You need both. And you need to believe you deserve what you're buildin'.
scenes.visual_prompt: Urban rooftop at sunrise, city skyline emerging from fog, warm golden light cutting through haze. No character. Contemplative vastness. Dark steel palette with warm dawn accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: We playin' the long game. Not the quick flip. Not the overnight success story. The kind where you build so solid, what you made outlives you. Generational. The kind of wealth they can't foreclose on, can't repo, can't delete with an algorithm.
scenes.visual_prompt: Railroad tracks stretching into golden distance, converging at vanishing point. Warm light. Historical weight but hopeful. Dark steel palette with warm rust accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

remotion_props.quote: Spoke some things into the **universe** and they **appeared**.

scenes.narration_text: The highest thing you can do is inspire. Not motivate — that wear off. Inspire. Light somethin' inside somebody that don't go out when you leave the room. That's the currency that matters. That's the investment that compounds forever. Luck is just bein' prepared when the door opens. Stay ready. The door always comes.
scenes.visual_prompt: Community garden in formerly empty urban lot, raised beds with vegetables growing through chain-link fence border, hand-painted sign reading 'COMMUNITY PLOT — TAKE WHAT YOU NEED', single sunflower taller than the fence, late afternoon golden light casting long shadows, freshly turned soil in new beds being prepared. No humans. Dark steel palette with warm golden-green accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

remotion_props.quote: The **highest** thing you can do is **inspire**.

scenes.narration_text: There's a proverb. Goes back to when our people were property. When learnin' to read was a crime. South Carolina, 1740 — made it illegal to teach a slave to write. Virginia. Alabama. Same laws. Same fear. They knew if we could read, we could think. And if we could think, we'd never stay chained.
scenes.visual_prompt: Dark interior lit by single candle on rough-hewn wooden table, leather-bound book open to handwritten pages, quill pen and dried ink beside it, letters scratched into the wooden table surface, warm amber candlelight creating intimate glow, reverent and quiet, historical weight without exploitation. Dark steel palette with warm amber accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.
scenes.notes: Voice drops lower. Historical register. Dates listed like indictments.

scenes.narration_text: So they passed knowledge in secret. Whispered lessons in the fields. Scratched letters in the dirt. Each person who learned turned around and taught another. One by one. In the dark. At risk of death. Each one teach one. That's how knowledge survived when the system tried to kill it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Extreme close-up of aged open book, yellowed pages with faded handwritten text, another hand's annotations in margins, dust motes caught in warm amber light beam, wax seal partially visible on page edge, surface beneath book is rough dirt floor with finger-drawn letters visible, reverent candlelight atmosphere. Dark steel palette with warm amber candlelight accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.
scenes.notes: Getting quieter. Near-whisper on 'In the dark. At risk of death.' Title spoken for first time.

remotion_props.quote: Each one **teach** one. That's how **knowledge** survived.

scenes.narration_text: That's what this channel is. A barbershop workshop that never closes. Each one teach one — but louder. Not a course. Not a brand deal. Not a rented Lamborghini in the thumbnail sellin' you a dream I ain't even livin'. This is witness testimony. From somebody who slept in a Cutlass Supreme and read tax codes by flashlight. And decided the people who look like him deserve to know what he learned.
scenes.visual_prompt: Urban intersection at dawn, barbershop on corner with lights still on inside from a late-night session, whiteboard visible through window, 'OPEN' sign still lit, first morning light breaking over rooftops, fresh wheat-paste poster on utility pole, empty street with sense of possibility, warm dawn colors breaking through dark steel sky. Dark steel palette with warm dawn accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: This ain't fear. Ain't hype. Ain't motivation that wear off by Wednesday. This is what I know. Delivered how I know it. In the voice I grew up with. For the people nobody else is talkin' to.
scenes.visual_prompt: Urban street at night, row of storefronts — one boarded up, one with faded 'GOING OUT OF BUSINESS' sign, one with hand-painted 'WE'RE STILL HERE' on plywood, single working streetlight casting orange pool on wet pavement, bus stop with no bench just a pole and sign, real and unglamorous. No humans. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: So let's talk about what you can own. Startin' from nothin'. No trust fund. No connections. No degree required.
scenes.visual_prompt: Concrete stoop transformed into teaching perch, worn steps with notebook and pen left on top step, hand-chalked '5 THINGS' on sidewalk pavement below, warm streetlight overhead casting circle of light like a spotlight, dawn light starting to appear on horizon behind buildings, energy shifting from night to morning. Dark steel palette, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Number one. A skill nobody can automate. I'm not talkin' about learning to code — everybody say that. And half of 'em gonna be out a job in two years 'cause the AI write better code than they do. I'm talkin' about somethin' you do with your hands or your mind that a machine can't replicate. Cuttin' hair. Readin' a room. Fixin' what's broke. Negotiatin' when the numbers don't add up. A barber in my neighborhood been cuttin' hair forty years. Ain't never worried about a robot takin' his chair. Know why? 'Cause people don't sit in his chair for the haircut. They sit there for the conversation. That's yours. Nobody can download it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Decades-old barbershop interior, worn leather chair polished smooth from forty years of use, wall covered in faded photographs from different eras — polaroids mixed with newer prints, hand-lettered 'SINCE 1984' sign above mirror, community service awards and framed newspaper clippings on wall, straight razor on folded towel, talc powder tin with vintage label, warm golden light from fixtures that haven't changed in decades. No humans. Warm golden tones on dark steel base, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Number two. A side business. Don't gotta be big. A pressure washin' rig. A notary service. Somethin' that got your name on it. I know a dude who started sellin' plates outta his mama kitchen. Now he got two food trucks and a catering contract with the city. He own that.
scenes.visual_prompt: Two food trucks parked side by side in urban lot at night, both with matching hand-painted branding, serving windows lit with warm light, steam rising from grills, hand-painted menu board with home-style food items, catering-size aluminum pans stacked near truck, city permit sticker visible on window, warm entrepreneurial energy. No humans. Dark steel palette with warm food-truck light accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Number three. Intellectual property. That sound complicated but it ain't. You write a course? That's IP. You record a tutorial? IP. You build a template other folk use? IP. The internet let you sell what you know to people you'll never meet. That's ownership without inventory.
scenes.visual_prompt: Close-up of laptop screen showing course platform dashboard with modules and progress bars, warm desk lamp, notebook with handwritten notes. No humans. Dark steel palette with warm screen glow accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Number four. A rental arrangement. I ain't talkin' about buyin' a buildin' — that come later. I'm talkin' about rentin' somethin' you control. A room. A parkin' spot. A storage unit you lease to somebody else. You don't need a mortgage. You need leverage.
scenes.visual_prompt: Small urban property at early morning, hand-written 'ROOM FOR RENT' sign in ground-floor window, separate 'PARKING — $75/MO' sign on chain-link fence around small lot, storage unit row visible in background with padlocks, brick building with fire escape, modest but controlled — evidence of small-scale ownership generating income. No humans. Dark steel palette with warm dawn accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Number five. Community equity. This the one folk sleep on. When your name mean somethin' on your block — when people trust you, send they kids to you, call you first when somethin' need fixin' — that's equity. Can't put it on a balance sheet. But it's the reason some folk can buy the plaza. The community back 'em 'cause they backed the community first.
scenes.visual_prompt: Small urban plaza at late afternoon golden hour, community bulletin board covered in thank-you notes and event flyers, barbershop with 'COMMUNITY MEETING TONIGHT' sign in window, children's basketball hoop mounted on utility pole, hand-painted mural on brick wall depicting neighborhood history, sense of a place where everyone knows your name. No humans. Dark steel palette with warm golden-green accent, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.

scenes.narration_text: Ninety-two percent of Black households hold zero business equity. Zero.

remotion_props.label: of Black households hold zero business equity

remotion_props.quote: None of these require venture capital.

scenes.narration_text: The hood already taught me what survival look like. What it feel like to stretch ten dollars into a week. What it mean to watch doors close and figure out which window to climb through. AI just made it urgent for everybody else. [2 second pause] Each one teach one. The marathon continues. Pay attention.
scenes.visual_prompt: Urban rooftop at pre-dawn, city skyline with lights beginning to fade as first warm light appears on eastern horizon, concrete ledge at roof edge, scattered evidence of someone spending time here — empty coffee cup, notebook, the remnants of a late night of thinking, sky transitioning from dark steel to warm dawn orange, vastness and possibility. Dark steel palette with subtle warm dawn, 2D cel-shaded style, film grain 20%, 16:9.
scenes.vo_split_marker: AI just made it urgent for everybody else.
scenes.notes: Split into 2 VO files at '[2 second pause]'. Pause added in FFMPEG assembly, not TTS.
